title: Command Generation Loader Decoupling
goal: Implement #433 by making generic command-generation code load and validate explicit IR/schema paths without importing Agentic Workspace contract tooling.
non_goals: Do not remove Agentic Workspace contract tooling.
Do not change command-package IR semantics.
Do not move runtime primitives into the generator.
Do not add first-contact product surfaces.

intent.outcome: Generic generation and static checks can consume arbitrary command-package IR and schema paths.
intent.constraints: Agentic Workspace-specific imports remain only in wrapper or consumer integration surfaces.
intent.latitude: Add a generic loader module and update root wrappers/checks to pass explicit paths.
intent.escalation: Escalate if validation requires workspace runtime semantics.

execution.next_step: Archive the completed execplan, commit, push, close #433, then continue #434.
execution.proof: proof selector plus generated package, contract tooling, ruff, planning, and focused tests.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: #430 isolate generic command-generation machinery for future extraction

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Generic package can validate and render command-package IR without workspace contract-tooling imports.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: #434 consumer wrapper clarity and #435 lift-out criteria.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: The package build metadata also needed to include schemas so the boundary is not source-tree-only.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Make the package streamlined, lightweight, frictionless, cheap, modular, and small while preserving leverage.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Dogfood package surfaces, use off-the-shelf validation where practical, commit/push milestone, close issue only after proof.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact loader API shape and wrapper check placement.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A change would make the generic package depend on Agentic Workspace runtime internals.

active_milestone.scope: generic IR loader and wrapper/check decoupling

immediate_next_action: Archive completed plan and close #433 after commit/push.
validation_commands: uv run ruff check src tests scripts packages/command-generation/src

uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed packages/command-generation/src/agentic_command_generation/ir.py packages/command-generation/src/agentic_command_generation/__init__.py packages/command-generation/pyproject.toml packages/command-generation/README.md scripts/generate/generate_command_packages.py scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py tests/test_contract_tooling.py uv.lock .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/command-generation-loader-decoupling.plan.json --format json
uv run ruff check src tests
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
completion_criteria: Generic command-generation package exposes a loader that validates explicit IR/schema paths.
Repo generator wrapper no longer imports agentic_workspace.contract_tooling.
Generated package static and conformance checks use the generic loader for IR validation.
Package schemas are included in package build metadata.

execution_run.handoff source: GitHub issue #433 plus compact planning summary
execution_run.what happened: Added agentic_command_generation.ir.load_command_package_ir, switched repo generation and static checks to explicit IR/schema paths, added tests proving the wrapper uses the generic loader, and included schemas in command-generation package metadata.
execution_run.scope touched: command-generation internal package, command package generator/check wrappers, contract tooling tests, planning state
execution_run.changed surfaces: generic package API, build metadata, wrapper/check code, active execplan
execution_run.validations run: Generated package freshness/static/conformance/Docker/Docker-conformance, contract tooling surfaces, workspace CLI tests, ruff, planning doctor/check, and package build.
execution_run.result for continuation: #433 is complete; #434 remains for consumer-boundary clarity.
execution_run.next step: Archive this plan, commit, push, close #433, refresh external evidence, then promote #434.

proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --conformance --require-node; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --docker --require-docker; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --docker-conformance --require-docker; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q; uv run pytest tests/test_contract_tooling.py -q; uv run ruff check src tests; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json; uv build --package agentic-command-generation

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Make command-generation code consume explicit file paths or validated payloads rather than importing Agentic Workspace-specific loaders for generic operation.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: The generic package owns load_command_package_ir(ir_path, schema_path), the repo generator wrapper imports that loader instead of agentic_workspace.contract_tooling, and generated package checks validate through the same loader.

closure_check.why this decision is honest: The bounded slice is complete and remaining intent is routed to a checked-in continuation owner.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Prepared closeout records intent satisfaction, closure decision, proof evidence, and distillation buckets.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Reopen when .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml roadmap activates a fresh bounded slice.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Generic loader/check boundary for command-package IR.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): README and tests only; no Memory update needed.

signals found: Schema directory was not included in command-generation package build metadata.
signals fixed: Added /schemas to package sdist include and wheel force-include.

discard.summary: Command-by-command execution detail stays in the archived proof record.

memory.summary: README and tests only; no Memory update needed.

config_check.summary: README and tests only; no Memory update needed.
